questions.clue: (I'm M.J. Acosta from NBC 7 San Diego.) This man resided at the Hotel Del Coronado for months at a time & wrote parts of several books here including "Dorothy and the Wizard in Oz"
(Hi, this is Ryan Kristafer from News 8.) Harriet Beecher Stowe lived next door to this other great American author at the time he wrote "A Connecticut Yankee in King Arthur's Court"
(I'm Juliana Mazza with 22News.) Leaping from the pages of the Dr. Seuss memorial sculpture are Thing 1 & Thing 2, agents of mayhem from this 1957 classic
(Hi, I'm Mark Allan from WDTN.) In his poem "Sympathy", Paul Laurence Dunbar wrote "I know why the caged bird sings"... giving this other African-American poet the title of her 1969 memoir
(Hi. I'm Ryan Kristafer from News 8.) This author of "Uncle Tom's Cabin" lived in this Hartford home from 1873 until her death in 1896
Dickens came to fame at age 24 with his first novel, about this club's "papers"
In 1960 this Dickens novel about a foundling was turned into a musical play
Charles met this "Moonstone" author & writing partner through a man named Augustus Egg
A 2012 bicentennial exhibition featured the manuscript of this unfinished mystery
Profession of Ellen Ternan, with whom Dickens became infatuated in 1857 when he was 45 & she, 18
This author hung out at Les Deux Magots in Paris, & wrote of drinking there with James Joyce in "A Movable Feast"
(Kelly of the Clue Crew reports from a museum in Dublin, Ireland.) I'm holding this novelist's cane in an Irish museum dedicated to him; he wrote passionately of Dublin but died in exile
In Bath, England, tours devoted to her feature places she wrote about in "Persuasion" & "Northanger Abbey"
You can visit the Moscow house where this author & some of his 13—"count" 'em—kids spent time from 1882 to 1901
Stood up at the bar of Paris' Hotel de Crillon? Oh well, so was Jake Barnes in this Hemingway novel
choices: The Old Man and the Sea
